data.title: KORE joins Russell Microcap index
data.description:
• Kore Group Holdings (NYSE:KORE) has been added to the Russell Microcap Index following the 2025 Russell indexes reconstitution, effective after the U.S. market opened on June 30. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Elanco in licensing pact with Australia’s Neurizon
data.description:
Australia-based biotech Neurizon Therapeutics (OTCPK:PAAFF) has inked an exclusive global licensing agreement with Elanco Animal Health (NYSE:ELAN) to obtain rights for monepantel, the active pharmaceutical ingredient in its lead asset NUZ-001.

Neurizon expects the deal to accelerate the development of NUZ-001, which is currently undergoing trials in humans for neurodegenerative diseases, including amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

As part of the agreement, Neurizon (OTCPK:PAAFF) will receive exclusive access to data and intellectual property rights related to monepantel by paying an undisclosed upfront licensing fee to the U.S. veterinary product firm.

Elanco (NYSE:ELAN) will also be eligible for $9.75M and $5.2M in development milestone payments for initial and subsequent licensed products, indications, etc., respectively. The company’s sales-based milestone payments are expected to reach up to $65M.

Additionally, there will be tiered single-digit royalties on global net sales of licensed products. With the companies focused on closing a supply agreement, a follow-up announcement on the deal is expected in H2 2025.

data.title: Broadridge to acquire European fund distribution firm Acolin, terms undisclosed
data.description:
Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) announced on Thursday an acquisition of the European cross-border fund distribution and regulatory services provider, Acolin.

Shares were -0.20% pre-market to $237.55.

The terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The acquisition is expected to facilitate Broadridge's expansion plans in Europe.

The transaction is expected to close in the first half of the 2026 fiscal year, and is not expected to have a material impact on the company's financial results.

data.title: Datadog being added to S&P 500 is 'validation moment' for company: Wedbush
data.description:
The inclusion of Datadog (NASDAQ:DDOG) to the S&P 500 (SP500) is a “validation moment” for the observability software company, Wedbush Securities said.

Shares rose 9.3% in premarket trading on Thursday.

“While the investment community largely expected Robinhood or AppLovin to join the index, DDOG was selected over these names as the company’s platform continues to gain momentum within the observability space, especially with AI front and center,” Wedbush analysts said in a note to clients.

They continued: “With the influx of agentic AI sidekicks, the company has focused intently on innovating through Bits AI as DDOG finds new opportunities to streamline the developer experience with products that leverage features like 24/7 background scanning to alert developers of issues, automated suggestions for bug fixes with summaries for traceability, and innovative stat monitoring methods.”

The investment firm, which raised its price target on Datadog to $170 from $140, added that Datadog is “well positioned” to keep gaining share in observability and its recent partnership with OpenAI is likely to compliment the agentic AI trends going across the globe.

Datadog will replace Juniper Networks after it was removed due to Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) completing its acquisition of the networking products provider. The change becomes effective on July 9.

data.title: FedEx gains after BNP Paribas flips from being a bear to a bull
data.description:
FedEx Corporation (NYSE:FDX) gained in early trading after BNP Paribas flipped from being a bear on the shipping stock to being a bull. Analyst Hugo Watkins and his team think the stock is oversold considering FedEx (NYSE:FDX) is outperforming on a relative basis on both volume and yield growth

BNP Paribas thinks the relative operational outperformance to rival United Parcel Service (UPS) will continue because UPS is facing company-specific headwinds.

Despite the U.S. domestic parcel market being competitive and Amazon (AMZN) continuing to present a major challenge to incumbents, FedEx (FDX) is seen stringing together some solid earnings reports. 

The firm moved to an Outperform rating on FedEx (FDX) after having it set at Underperform and assigned a new price target of $270. 

The Wall Street analyst scorecard on FedEx (FDX) shows 21 Buy-equivalent ratings, 8 Hold-equivalent ratings, and 3 Sell-equivalent ratings on the books. Meanwhile, Seeking Alpha analysts have a consensus Hold rating on the stock.

Shares of FedEx (FDX) rose 1.3% in premarket action to $242.33.

data.title: BlackRock considers selling Saudi Aramco gas pipelines stake - report
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), the giant asset manager, is weighing the sale of its interest in the leasing rights to Saudi Aramco's (ARMCO) natural gas pipeline network back to the energy behemoth, according to a media report published on Thursday.

The two companies are in talks for a potential transaction, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) will also consider other options for the stake if the talks don't result in an agreement, according ot some of the people.

BlackRock (BLK) acquired the stake in the assets in 2021, which is likely to be valued at billions of dollars, the report said. A group led by BlackRock (BLK) had invested $15.5B to buy 49% of the entity that holds leasing rights over the pipelines.

In a similar transaction that year, Aramco (ARMCO) sold a $12.4B stake related to its oil pipelines, the article said. The two deals, among the energy giant's largest sales, reflect Saudi Arabia's shift to sell assets to develop new industries such as electric vehicles and artificial intelligence.

No final decisions have been made on BlackRock's (BLK) stake in the natural gas pipeline leasing rights, and there's no guarantee the talks will lead to a deal, Bloomberg said.

data.title: Polestar Automotive signs a deal to make a new SUV in Europe
data.description:
Polestar (NASDAQ:PSNY) announced on Thursday that it is taking the next step in diversifying its contract manufacturing footprint by expanding it to Europe. The Sweden-based company inked a memorandum of understanding with Volvo Cars (OTCPK:VLVOF) to have Polestar 7 manufactured in Kosice, Slovakia. The premium compact SUV is expected to be launched in 2028. Construction started on the Volvo Cars factory in Kosice in 2023. 

Polestar 7 will be developed in accordance with the company’s recently announced strategy to use architectures from within the Geely Holding Group (OTCPK:GELYY). Polestar 7 will utilize a technology base from Volvo Cars (), benefiting from group component sharing, cell-to-body technology with next-generation battery density and performance, as well as the next generation of in-house developed e-motors. 

Weighing in on the development, Polestar (NASDAQ:PSNY) CEO Michael Lohscheller called working with Volvo Cars (OTCPK:VLVOF) to develop and manufacture Polestar 7 in Europe is a unique opportunity that will strengthen the company's position in its home market. "Our strategy of utilizing Group architectures as the base for our future model line-up gives us access to the best, latest technologies, in a cost-efficient manner," added Lohscheller.  

The Polestar 7 is positioned as a premium compact SUV, targeting the fastest-growing and most profitable segment in the electric vehicle market. The model is designed to compete directly with models like the Porsche Macan Electric (OTCPK:VLKAF), Tesla Model Y (TSLA), and other high-end compact EVs.

Shares of Polestar (PSNY) rose 1.8% in premarket trading on Thursday to $1.11 vs. the 52-week range of $0.61 to $1.94. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on PSNY is flashing Hold.

data.title: Societe Generale launches Japan bank basket amid sector outperformance
data.description:
Japanese bank stocks have outperformed the broader stock market, helped by a new price regime, improving profitability and elevated shareholder returns, said Societe Generale, adding it maintains its bullish views.  

Societe Generale also introduced the SG Japan Bank Basket.

Japanese bank stocks have outperformed the TOPIX by almost 90%, helped by a number of factors. 

The expansion in labor supply since the start of Abenomics is peaking, which has led to wage growth, according to Societe Generale's strategist, Frank Benzimra.

Abenomics refers to economic policies implemented in Japan by Prime Minister Shinzo Abe starting in 2012.

"With rising inflation and improving domestic demand, the Bank of Japan (BoJ) has started to normalize monetary policy. We expect rate hikes to continue," Benzimra added.

"The labor force is declining, triggering some wage growth recovery. As a result, the BoJ has dismantled the ultra-loose monetary policy framework, and domestic firms are coming back to Japan, prioritizing domestic capex over overseas capital investments."

Banks are also benefiting from rising interest rates and also growing domestic demand, leading to an increase in bank lending. 

Total shareholder yields, combining dividends and share buybacks, are close to 4.3% presently.

As part of the SG Japan Bank Basket, Societe Generale has assigned 80% to megabanks, as they have diversified sources of profit compared to regional banks, and they face lower risks of valuation losses from rising JGB yields.

The basket tracks the performance of just 20 names and excludes illiquid small-cap stocks, Societe Generale said.

data.title: Novartis fails in late-stage trial for Cosentyx in giant cell arteritis
data.description:
Novartis (NYSE:NVS) (OTCPK:NVSEF) announced Thursday that its FDA-approved anti-inflammatory agent Cosentyx failed to reach the main goal in a Phase 3 trial for adults with giant cell arteritis ((GCA), a vascular disorder.

Citing initial data from its Phase 3 GCAptAIN study, the Swiss drugmaker said Cosentyx as part of a combination therapy didn't succeed in causing a statistically significant improvement in sustained remission at Week 52.

The global placebo-controlled trial was designed to evaluate the biologic with a glucocorticoid taper regimen in patients with newly diagnosed or relapsing GCA. An inflammatory condition affecting mainly those over 50 years of age, GCA can cause permanent vision loss and aortic aneurysms.

While the study didn’t demonstrate statistical superiority for secondary goals, Cosentyx, approved in multiple countries for conditions such as plaque psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis, was found to have a safety profile in GCA consistent with past data, Novartis (NYSE:NVS) said.

The company expects to complete a full analysis of GCAptAIN data and disclose the results in the future.

data.title: Samsung is said to be delaying completion of US chip plant due to lack of customers
data.description:
Samsung's (OTCPK:SSNLF) second U.S. chip plant, originally scheduled to begin operations in 2024, has been delayed until 2026 due to a shortage of local customers, according to Nikkei Asia.

The South Korean chipmaker has said it will invest more than $37 billion in Texas in the coming years, having been awarded a grant by the Biden administration of up to $4.7 billion in December under the CHIPS and Science Act.

"The process [of completing the Taylor fab] is delayed because there are no customers. [Samsung] is not in a situation where it can do something, even if it brings equipment in at the moment," a source close to the situation said.

A chip supply chain executive familiar with the matter also told Nikkei Asia that Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF), which already makes chips in Austin, Texas, is in no hurry to install chipmaking equipment in the new plant.

Samsung’s delay in completing its U.S. chip plant highlights the broader difficulties semiconductor companies encounter when expanding globally. TSMC (TSM) faced similar hurdles—including construction setbacks and labor shortages—during the development of its first advanced facility in Arizona.

Despite those challenges, the plant began mass production late last year and has since landed major AI chip clients, including Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD), Amazon (AMZN), and Google (GOOG).

Analysts say Samsung's contract chipmaking business is also struggling with yield, a key metric for assessing production quality in chip manufacturing.

"Samsung Foundry faced unstable yields and order losses. Although yields have since improved, U.S. restrictions on high-end chip production for China have further weighed on the company, keeping its capacity utilization below the industry average," Joanne Chiao, a Trendforce analyst, told Nikkei Asia.
